summary: End-to-end backend + frontend testing of the 10 Cherry Case fixes. Created /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py with 24 tests covering admin verify/login, admin add-stars, crash distribution + idempotent cashout, slots RTP, mines size/RTP. 23/24 passed. Frontend Playwright run validated promo input, admin modal, admin page, mines 3/5/7-only, Profile admin-hint removed, RU translations on Games, no hardcoded English on Crash.

minor.issue: Over 1000 sequential calls saw 2 consecutive identical crash_at values. Likely caused by another concurrent client mutating the global _last_crash_multiplier between our sequential calls (single uvicorn worker, but shared public endpoint). Per-stream non-repeat property is preserved; cross-stream is not.

action_items: (Optional) Make crash_multiplier no-repeat per-user instead of global to avoid cross-stream coincidence (track last value in user/game doc).
critical_code_review_comments: generate_crash_multiplier uses a module-level _last_crash_multiplier; safer per-user state or DB-backed last-value for true non-repeat guarantee under concurrency.
/api/profile route is referenced by legacy test_cherry_case.py but does not exist (returns 404). server.py exposes /api/me. Either add a /profile alias or update legacy tests.
MinesRevealReq uses 'index' field; legacy test_cherry_case.py sends 'cell' — those reveal calls silently get 422 in legacy suite. Not a regression of the new fixes but worth noting.
server.py file is 1141 lines — consider splitting routers (admin, games, cases) into modules for maintainability.
test_credentials: ADMIN_SECRET=9hNiTxKMM9Qb9YB9zJgqPhuVpS3MUrlkY4LtV3wVU-k (in backend/.env); test users auto-created via /api/auth/telegram fallback.
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_<uuid> users via /api/auth/telegram during pytest runs. Each gets demo_balance=1,000,000 by default; some topped up to real-balance for admin add-stars tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend reachable at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Use /api/me (not /api/profile). Mines reveal payload uses 'index' (not 'cell'). Admin endpoints work with header X-ADMIN or POST body {secret}. Crash multiplier sampler is global, so consecutive-repeat assertions can be flaky if other test clients are running. Frontend TopBar exposes data-testid promo-input/promo-apply-btn/admin-modal*; Admin page exposes add-stars-* and toggle-users-btn/users-list. RU translations validated on Games and Crash.
rca of the issue: Crash repeat: _last_crash_multiplier is module-global. With a single uvicorn worker, sequential requests from one client should never repeat. But the public preview URL receives traffic from other clients too — if another client calls /games/crash/start between our two sequential samples, it can update _last to X, our next sample matches our previous (now-stale) value and is accepted. Mitigation: track last_crash_at per user (and/or store last_n in DB and reject repeats inside a window per user).
